heading: Transfers of franchises, trademarks, and trade names
label: 1253 Transfers of franchises, trademarks, and trade names

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: A transfer of a franchise, trademark, or trade name shall not be treated as a sale or exchange of a capital asset if the transferor retains any significant power, right, or continuing interest with respect to the subject matter of the franchise, trademark, or trade name.

children.heading: Franchise
children.label: 1 Franchise
children.text: The term “franchise” includes an agreement which gives one of the parties to the agreement the right to distribute, sell, or provide goods, services, or facilities, within a specified area.

children.heading: Significant power, right, or continuing interest
children.label: 2 Significant power, right, or continuing interest
A
children.text: A right to disapprove any assignment of such interest, or any part thereof.

children.label: B
children.text: A right to terminate at will.

children.label: C
children.text: A right to prescribe the standards of quality of products used or sold, or of services furnished, and of the equipment and facilities used to promote such products or services.

children.label: D
children.text: A right to require that the transferee sell or advertise only products or services of the transferor.

children.label: E
children.text: A right to require that the transferee purchase substantially all of his supplies and equipment from the transferor.

children.label: F
children.text: A right to payments contingent on the productivity, use, or disposition of the subject matter of the interest transferred, if such payments constitute a substantial element under the transfer agreement.

children.heading: Transfer
children.label: 3 Transfer
children.text: The term “transfer” includes the renewal of a franchise, trademark, or trade name.

children.heading: Treatment of contingent payments by transferor
children.label: c Treatment of contingent payments by transferor
children.text: Amounts received or accrued on account of a transfer, sale, or other disposition of a franchise, trademark, or trade name which are contingent on the productivity, use, or disposition of the franchise, trademark, or trade name transferred shall be treated as amounts received or accrued from the sale or other disposition of property which is not a capital asset.

children.heading: Treatment of payments by transferee
children.label: d Treatment of payments by transferee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any amount described in subparagraph (B) which is paid or incurred during the taxable year on account of a transfer, sale, or other disposition of a franchise, trademark, or trade name shall be allowed as a deduction under section 162(a) (relating to trade or business expenses).

children.heading: Amounts to which paragraph applies
children.label: B Amounts to which paragraph applies
i
children.text: is contingent on the productivity, use, or disposition of the franchise, trademark, or trade name, and

children.label: I
children.text: which are payable not less frequently than annually throughout the entire term of the transfer agreement, and

children.label: II
children.text: which are substantially equal in amount (or payable under a fixed formula).

children.heading: Other payments
children.label: 2 Other payments
children.text: Any amount paid or incurred on account of a transfer, sale, or other disposition of a franchise, trademark, or trade name to which paragraph (1) does not apply shall be treated as an amount chargeable to capital account.

children.heading: Renewals, etc.
children.label: 3 Renewals, etc.
children.text: For purposes of determining the term of a transfer agreement under this section, there shall be taken into account all renewal options (and any other period for which the parties reasonably expect the agreement to be renewed).
